Title: Unexpected Dual Function of Plant YUCCA Enzymes Links Chlorophyll Catabolism to Auxin Homeostasis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavin-containing monooxygenase (YUCCA family); FAD-dependent group B FPMO/BVMO-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted transmembrane helix; vacuolar localization reported in berry flesh
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with PPH, PaO, and TIC55; detected in aging yellow-green leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected from grapevine aging yellow-green leaf lysate
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chlorophyll catabolism (late phyllobilin branch); auxin (IAA) biosynthesis via IPA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late-stage chlorophyll catabolite deformylation branch at PleB/PxB/PrB level; IPA-to-IAA step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Late-stage branch step in chlorophyll degradation; rate-limiting key step in auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: PleB (e.g., Vv-PleB-57, Po-PleB 1n); Vv-PxB/Cj-PxB-2; Cj-PrB; indole-3-pyruvate (IPA, 15)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of chlorophyll catabolites followed by hydrolytic deformylation/γ-lactam formation; also converts indole-3-pyruvate to IAA via a revised BVMO-type oxygenation mechanism
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Catalyzes the rate-limiting key step in IAA biosynthesis; for chlorophyll catabolism identified as a previously missing late-step enzyme in Vitis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms deformylated chlorophyll catabolites and directly forms IAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll catabolites are associated with senescence/nutrient relocation; IAA controls plant growth and delays senescence, fruit ripening, and abscission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces major deformylated phyllobilins in grapevine and simultaneously supports IAA biosynthesis; chlorophyll catabolite product DPleB inhibits the IAA-forming activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affinity pull-down proteomics; co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in E. coli BL21; purified enzyme assays with NADPH/NADH; HR-MS/MS and UV-vis product identification; 18O2 labeling; substrate competition and inhibition assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf senescence/aging yellow-green leaves; in vitro NADPH or NADH supply; 18O2 atmosphere; pre-incubation with DPleB
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics pull-down LC-MS; transcriptomic co-expression database ATTED-II; genome assembly ASM3070453v1; UniProt proteome UP000009183
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PPH; PaO; TIC55 (co-expression/pull-down association, not direct physical interaction proven)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product and/or substrate inhibition complicated kinetic analysis; high chlorophyll catabolite levels may strongly suppress auxin biosynthesis during senescence
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune the balance between chlorophyll breakdown and auxin homeostasis in perennial crops, but species-specific dual activity and feedback inhibition must be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not efficiently accept 3^2-substituted chlorophyll catabolites (glycosylated/malonylated forms); did not convert cyclohexanone or phenylpyruvate; likely explains branching of PaO/phyllobilin pathway in Vitis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvYUCCA10 was identified by chlorophyll-catabolite pull-down plus co-expression mining as the missing late-stage chlorophyll catabolic enzyme in grapevine. 2. Purified VvYUCCA10 deformylated PleB/PxB/PrB-type phyllobilins through a Baeyer-Villiger oxidation/hydrolysis mechanism confirmed by 18O2 incorporation. 3. The same enzyme also catalyzed the canonical IPA-to-IAA step, but physiological-range chlorophyll catabolites inhibited IAA formation, linking chlorophyll catabolism to auxin homeostasis during senescence.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavin-containing monooxygenase (YUCCA family); FAD-dependent BVMO-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chlorophyll catabolism; auxin (IAA) biosynthesis via IPA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late-stage phyllobilin deformylation branch; IPA-to-IAA step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Late-stage branch step in chlorophyll degradation; IAA biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes deformylation of PleB-type chlorophyll catabolites and also converts IPA to IAA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supports the rate-limiting YUCCA step in IAA biosynthesis; chlorophyll-catabolite role newly demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms deformylated chlorophyll catabolites and directly forms IAA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chlorophyll catabolites are senescence-associated; IAA regulates growth and senescence timing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displays dual enzymatic role spanning chlorophyll catabolite deformylation and IAA biosynthesis in asterid coffee
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pBLAST homolog selection; heterologous expression in E. coli BL21; lysate and purified-enzyme assays; HR-MS detection of products; leaf lysate metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro NADPH-dependent assays; leaf tissue context
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In vivo significance requires further genetic validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for engineering senescence-associated hormone/catabolite balance in perennial species, but function is clade/species dependent
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of four Coffea arabica YUCCA10 variants showed close similarity to VvYUCCA10; coffee leaves contained deformylated phyllobilins but no detectable IPA/IAA under the assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaYUCCA10, a close homolog of VvYUCCA10, deformylated PleB substrates to DPleBs in vitro. 2. CaYUCCA10 also converted IPA to IAA, confirming dual YUCCA10 function beyond rosids. 3. Detection of deformylated phyllobilins in coffee leaves supports physiological relevance of this chlorophyll-catabolic activity in coffee.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reported to be highly expressed in siliques at different stages of senescence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: A. thaliana siliques during senescence were examined
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Rate-limiting key step in auxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts IPA to IAA; did not deformylate tested PleB chlorophyll catabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Catalyzes the rate-limiting key step in IAA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and developmental processes; delays senescence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Retains canonical auxin biosynthetic activity but lacks the chlorophyll-catabolite deformylation activity observed for VvYUCCA10 and CaYUCCA10
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli BL21; in vitro enzyme assays comparing IPA and PleB substrates; product ratio analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay conditions; senescing siliques profiled for metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Species-specific specialization suggests that transferring YUCCA10 functions across species may not reproduce chlorophyll-catabolism effects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an auxin biosynthesis component, but not suitable based on this study for PleB deformylation engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtYUCCA10 produced IAA faster than the tested perennial homologs and lacked a conserved Tyr present in VvYUCCA10/CaYUCCA10 models
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtYUCCA10 converted IPA to IAA efficiently and faster than the tested Vitis and Coffea homologs. 2. AtYUCCA10 did not accept Vv-PleB-57 or Po-PleB 1n as chlorophyll-catabolite substrates. 3. The results demonstrate species-specific divergence within YUCCA10 enzymes, with Arabidopsis retaining canonical auxin activity but lacking the newly identified chlorophyll-catabolic role.
